channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Applied Materials to cut more than 1,400 jobs after sales slow
item.description: Earlier this month, Applied Materials said an expansion of US export curbs to China would cost the company $600 million in lost revenue in fiscal 2026.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: CISA warns Motex Landscope Endpoint Manager has a worrying security flaw, so patch now
item.description: The critical severity bug is being actively exploited in the wild, possibly by ransomware actors, CISA says.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shifting your organizational focus from MTTD to MTTC in 8 steps
item.description: I spend a lot of time with operations leaders who have excellent visibility … but disappointing outcomes. The paradigm is that we invest heavily in detection, correlation and dashboards, then depend on human heroics. It’s the smoke-alarm-without-a-sprinkler problem.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenAI is connecting all company secrets to ChatGPT - all your work data and apps are now open
item.description: Company knowledge, a self-explanatory ChatGPT tool, will cite internal documents for accurate reporting.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Bethesda celebrates Fallout Day with a host of announcements, including a Fallout 4 Anniversary Edition and Nintendo Switch 2 Edition, a native Fallout 76 release for PS5 and Xbox Series X, and more
item.description: Bethesda celebrates Fallout Day with a slew of announcements that include a Fallout 4 Anniversary Edition, Fallout 76 for current-gen consoles, and more.
item.dc:creator: Demi Williams, TechRadar

item.title: Intel faces high earnings bar as stock soars and cash pours in
item.description: Investors will be looking beyond the bottom line in chipmaker Intel Corp.'s third-quarter earnings after a wave of investments from the White House, Nvidia Corp. and Softbank Group Corp. sent the stock soaring 86% in less than three months.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: PyTorch team unveils framework for programming clusters
item.description: The PyTorch team at Meta, stewards of the PyTorch open source machine learning framework, has unveiled Monarch, a distributed programming framework intended to bring the simplicity of PyTorch to entire clusters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reddit sues AI company Perplexity and others for 'industrial-scale' scraping of user comments
item.description: Social media platform Reddit sued the artificial intelligence company Perplexity AI and three other entities on Wednesday, alleging their involvement in an “industrial-scale, unlawful” economy to “scrape” the comments of millions of Reddit users for commercial gain.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Florida test scores rise, attendance improves after cellphone ban
item.description: A new study finds that Florida test scores rose and attendance improved after a school cellphone ban, although short-term disciplinary problems also arose in the state.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Call to ban AI superintelligence could redraw the global tech race between the US and China
item.description: More than 850 prominent figures have called for a prohibition on developing AI superintelligence, a move that could reshape enterprise AI investments and intensify the US-China technology race if adopted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel’s Panther Lake chip could allow Windows 11 to morph into an agentic OS
item.description: Intel’s upcoming “Panther Lake” processor for AI PCs could be a key building block in the transition of Windows 11 into an AI agent-driven OS, analysts said this week. Panther Lake will be available in PCs starting early in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google kills its cookie killer
item.description: Privacy Sandbox, Google’s attempt to create an alternative to cookies, looks like it has reached the end of the line. The company has announced that it is discontinuing 11 Privacy Sandbox technologies — pretty much the entire gamut.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Anthropic extends Claude Code to browsers
item.description: Anthropic has launched Claude Code on the web, enabling developers to use the company’s AI coding assistant directly from their browser or smartphone, with no terminal required. Developers can connect a first GitHub repository to Claude Code on the web at claude.com/code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Visual Studio Code taps AI for merge conflict resolution
item.description: Visual Studio Code 1.105, the latest release of Microsoft’s popular Visual Studio Code editor, introduces several new AI coding features, including the ability to resolve merge conflicts with AI assistance, the ability to resume recent chat sessions, and the ability to install Model Context Protocol
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US court tells Israeli company to stop targeting WhatsApp
item.description: WASHINGTON, Oct 18 (Reuters) - A U.S. court has ordered Israel's NSO Group to stop targeting Meta Platforms WhatsApp messaging service, a development the spyware company warned could put it out of business. In a 25-page ruling handed down Friday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Raphael Satter, NewsNation
